div1.entryFree: Clear-stories, only by conjecture in our texts in Tw. IV, 2, 41: it hath bay windows transparent as barricadoes, and the c. towards the south north are as lustrous as ebony. F1 has cleer stores, the other Ff clear stones, which may be right. Clear-stories are, according to some, upper rows of windows, as in halls and churches; according to others, windows without a transom or cross-piece in the middle of them. A word of uncertain meaning and, at any rate, unknown to Shakespeare.
